channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Land Bank requests extension for corporate plan submission due to cyberattack
item.description: The Land Bank has been unable to finalise and table its 2026/27 corporate plan on time following a recent ransomware cyberattack that compromised its IT systems. Finance Minister Enoch Godongwana info...

item.title: Governance Reforms at Ontario Law Society Raise Concerns About Political Bias
item.description: The Law Society of Ontario (LSO), which oversees more than 60,000 lawyers and paralegals, recently mandated a six-hour Indigenous cultural training course. While education on Canada's history is valua...

item.title: Regina Pat Canadians secure Telus Cup berth with win over Moose Jaw
item.description: The Regina Pat Canadians have earned a spot in the 2026 Telus Cup following a decisive victory against the Moose Jaw Winmar Warriors. The win, which took place on April 5, 2026, showcased the Canadian...

item.title: DCI Reviews Gachagua’s Remarks on Fuel Scandal, Promises Legal Action if Laws Breached
item.description: Nairobi – The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has stated it will take action over statements made by former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua during an Easter Sunday service at AIPCA Gako...

item.title: Tori Spelling and Four Children Injured in California Car Accident After Red-Light Collision
item.description: Actress Tori Spelling and four of her children were taken to the hospital following a traffic accident in Temecula, California, on April 2, 2026. The crash occurred when another driver allegedly ran a...

item.title: Simple One-Pot Braised Chickpeas with Chorizo and Greens
item.description: This recipe offers a fuss-free, hearty dish that combines the smokiness of chorizo with tender chickpeas, perfect for a midweek meal or a winter warmer. The method is simple: onions are gently sautéed...

item.title: Pet Shop Boys Reflect on Iconic Visuals, Artistic Freedom, and Career Highlights
item.description: Pet Shop Boys, the British synth-pop duo, have long been celebrated not only for their music but also for their groundbreaking visual style. A new 600-page book, 'Pet Shop Boys: Volume', documents ove...

item.title: The Global Risks of Tactical Nuclear Weapons
item.description: Even a limited nuclear exchange, often referred to as a 'small nuclear war,' could have catastrophic consequences far beyond the battlefield. Tactical nuclear weapons, despite being lower yield than s...

item.title: Ruth Higgins SC appointed as Australia’s first female Solicitor-General
item.description: Australia has made history with the appointment of barrister Ruth Higgins SC as the nation’s first female Solicitor-General. Announced by Attorney-General Michelle Rowland, Dr Higgins brings over 30 y...

item.title: Moody’s revises India’s 2026-27 GDP growth estimate to 6% amid West Asia conflict
item.description: Global ratings agency Moody’s has revised India’s economic growth projection for the financial year 2026-27 from 6.8% to 6%, primarily due to disruptions caused by the ongoing conflict in West Asia. T...

item.title: DBCC to present fuel tax suspension recommendations to President Marcos
item.description: The Development Budget Coordination Committee (DBCC) is scheduled to meet with President Ferdinand “Bongbong” Marcos Jr. on April 7, 2026, to submit its recommendations regarding a possible suspension...

item.title: COA Appointed as World Trade Organization’s External Auditor
item.description: The Commission on Audit (COA) of the Philippines has been officially appointed as the new External Auditor for the World Trade Organization (WTO). This six-year, non-renewable term marks a significant...

item.title: Cabinet Declares Nigerian ‘Coronation’ in Eastern Cape Legally Invalid
item.description: Cabinet in South Africa has expressed strong disapproval regarding the recent so-called coronation of a Nigerian national as an alleged chief in KuGompo City, Eastern Cape. Speaking during a post-Cabi...

item.title: Transport Groups Cite Lack of Fuel Relief, Drivers Reduce Operations
item.description: Transport organizations in the Philippines have raised alarms over the growing difficulties faced by public utility drivers due to rising fuel prices and slow government support. Leaders from major gr...

item.title: Pastor Kanyari Criticises Boxing Match Outcome and Organiser Over Controversial Punch
item.description: Pastor Victor Kanyari has expressed his deep disappointment following the defeat of amateur boxer Mbavu Destroyer in a bout against Majembe, held at Kasarani International Sports Centre Indoor Arena o...

item.title: Eastern Cape police search for eight detainees who escaped custody
item.description: Eastern Cape police are conducting a comprehensive manhunt after eight detainees escaped from the Afsondering police station cells in Maluti on Saturday. The detainees, whose names have been released ...

item.title: UK Seeks Anthropic Expansion Amid U.S. Defence Dispute
item.description: The United Kingdom is actively trying to attract Anthropic, an artificial intelligence company, to increase its operations in the country. This initiative comes in the wake of a dispute between Anthro...

item.title: Leeds Reach FA Cup Semi-Finals After Dramatic Win Over West Ham
item.description: Leeds don reach FA Cup semi-finals for di first time in 39 years after dem survive one correct penalty shoot-out against West Ham. Di match for London Stadium no easy at all as Leeds dey enjoy two-goa...

item.title: DCCEEW pauses hiring and offers voluntary redundancies amid APS budget cuts
item.description: The Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water (DCCEEW) has announced a pause on new hiring and is offering voluntary redundancies as part of a broader cost-saving initiative acro...

item.title: Canada’s oldest person and WWII veteran Burdett Sisler passes away at 110
item.description: Burdett Sisler, believed to be Canada’s oldest person and the oldest surviving Second World War veteran, passed away on April 2 at the age of 110, just shy of his 111th birthday. Born in Akron, Ohio, ...
